data.name: CG001_Title
data.value: Missing parameterless constructor or constructor that initializes properties

data.name: CG001_MessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' implementing {1} must have a parameterless constructor or constructors that initialize all properties

data.name: CG001_Description
data.value: Types implementing INode or IRelationship must have a parameterless constructor or constructors that initialize their (get/set) properties.

data.name: CG002_Title
data.value: Property must have public getters and setters or initializers

data.name: CG002_MessageFormat
data.value: Property '{0}' in type '{1}' must have public getter and either public setter or public init accessor

data.name: CG002_Description
data.value: Properties in INode and IRelationship implementations must have public getters and either public setters or public property initializers.

data.name: CG003_Title
data.value: Property cannot be INode or IRelationship type

data.name: CG003_MessageFormat
data.value: Property '{0}' in type '{1}' cannot be of type '{2}' which implements INode or IRelationship

data.name: CG003_Description
data.value: Properties of types implementing INode or IRelationship cannot be INode or IRelationship or collections of them.

data.name: CG004_Title
data.value: Invalid property type for INode implementation

data.name: CG004_MessageFormat
data.value: Property '{0}' in INode type '{1}' has invalid type '{2}'. Properties must be simple types, complex types, collections of simple types, or collections of complex types

data.name: CG004_Description
data.value: Properties of INode implementations must be simple types, complex types, collections of simple types, or collections of complex types, applied recursively.

data.name: CG005_Title
data.value: Invalid property type for IRelationship implementation

data.name: CG005_MessageFormat
data.value: Property '{0}' in IRelationship type '{1}' has invalid type '{2}'. Properties must be simple types or collections of simple types

data.name: CG005_Description
data.value: Properties of IRelationship implementations must be simple types or collections of simple types.

data.name: CG006_Title
data.value: Complex type property contains graph interface types

data.name: CG006_MessageFormat
data.value: Complex type '{0}' used by property '{1}' in type '{2}' contains properties of INode or IRelationship types

data.name: CG006_Description
data.value: Properties of complex properties cannot be INode or IRelationship or collections of them. This rule is applied recursively.

data.name: CG007_Title
data.value: Duplicate PropertyAttribute label in type hierarchy

data.name: CG007_MessageFormat
data.value: Property '{0}' in type '{1}' uses PropertyAttribute label '{2}' which is already used by property '{3}' in base type '{4}'

data.name: CG007_Description
data.value: A type hierarchy cannot have PropertyAttribute annotations with the same Label value across all properties in that type hierarchy.

data.name: CG008_Title
data.value: Duplicate RelationshipAttribute label in type hierarchy

data.name: CG008_MessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' uses RelationshipAttribute label '{1}' which is already used by base type '{2}'

data.name: CG008_Description
data.value: A type hierarchy cannot have RelationshipAttribute annotations with the same Label value across all types in that type hierarchy.

data.name: CG009_Title
data.value: Duplicate NodeAttribute label in type hierarchy

data.name: CG009_MessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' uses NodeAttribute label '{1}' which is already used by base type '{2}'

data.name: CG009_Description
data.value: A type hierarchy cannot have NodeAttribute annotations with the same Label value across all types in that type hierarchy.

data.name: CG010_Title
data.value: Circular reference without nullable type

data.name: CG010_MessageFormat
data.value: Property '{0}' in type '{1}' creates a circular reference to type '{2}' without using a nullable type

data.name: CG010_Description
data.value: A type implementing INode or IRelationship cannot contain a type reference cycle without a nullable type.

data.name: CG011_Title
data.value: Type should inherit from base class instead of implementing interface directly

data.name: CG011_MessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' should inherit from '{1}' instead of implementing '{2}' directly. The base class provides default implementations for runtime metadata properties

data.name: CG011_Description
data.value: Types should inherit from Node or Relationship base classes instead of implementing INode or IRelationship directly. The base classes provide default implementations for runtime metadata properties like Labels and Type, which are managed by the graph provider.

data.name: CG012_Title
data.value: Attribute has no effect on a type that doesn't implement the matching interface

data.name: CG012_MessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' is annotated with [{1}] but does not implement {2}; the attribute has no effect

data.name: CG012_Description
data.value: NodeAttribute only affects types implementing INode, and RelationshipAttribute only affects types implementing IRelationship. Applying either attribute to a type that does not implement the matching interface is a silent no-op.

data.name: CG013_Title
data.value: Type cannot have both NodeAttribute and RelationshipAttribute

data.name: CG013_MessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' has both [Node] and [Relationship] attributes; a type can only be one or the other

data.name: CG013_Description
data.value: A type cannot be annotated with both NodeAttribute and RelationshipAttribute. Label resolution only honors one of the two, silently ignoring the other, so applying both is always a mistake.

data.name: CG014_Title
data.value: Graph entity types must be reference types

data.name: CG014_MessageFormat
data.value: {0} '{1}' implements {2}; graph entity types must be reference types (class or record class)

data.name: CG014_Description
data.value: Types implementing INode or IRelationship must be reference types. Struct value semantics (copy on assignment/parameter-pass) turn load-mutate-update patterns into silent lost-update bugs, and the covariant graph query surface relies on reference-type variance conversions that do not apply to value types.

data.name: CG015_Title
data.value: ComplexPropertyAttribute has no effect

data.name: CG015_MessageFormat
data.value: [ComplexProperty] on property '{0}' has no effect: {1}

data.name: CG015_Description
data.value: ComplexPropertyAttribute only configures graph-backed complex properties. It has no effect on simple values or simple collections, and an empty RelationshipType override silently falls back to the property name.
